description:
You are acting as a configuration-management assistant.  
        A brand-new, *already existing* configuration file is located at:

            /home/user/tracked/empty.conf

        • It is a **zero-byte** file (completely empty).  
        • Your goal is to record its SHA-256 checksum in a log file called  

            /home/user/config_checksums.log  

          located in the user’s home directory.

        Required result:
        ------------------------------------------------------------
        1. Create (or overwrite if it already exists) the file
           /home/user/config_checksums.log.

        2. Write **exactly one line** to that file.  
           That line must contain:
              a) the literal string “empty.conf”  
              b) two blank space characters  
              c) the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the file

           The required final line – including its *single* trailing newline –
           must look exactly like this (pay special attention to the two
           spaces between the filename and the digest):

           empty.conf  e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855\n

        3. Use a single, concise terminal command to accomplish the task.

        Verification notes (what the automated grader checks):
        • /home/user/config_checksums.log exists.  
        • Its size corresponds to exactly the 82 characters shown above
          (81 visible characters plus the terminating newline).
        • The content matches the line printed above, byte-for-byte.

        Do NOT add any extra spaces, tabs, or lines.  
        Do NOT print anything else to the log file.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
        • Path: /home/user/tracked/empty.conf
        • State: 0-byte regular file
        • SHA-256 digest (hex): e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

        Post-task expectations:
        • /home/user/config_checksums.log exists with permissions 0644
          (rw-r--r--) and contains exactly:

          empty.conf␠␠e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855␊

          where ␠ denotes a normal ASCII space and ␊ denotes the single
          newline (ASCII 0x0A) that terminates the line.
